intro.headline: Build Your Team Identity
intro.body: A team charter is a living artifact that captures who you are as a team. This workshop guides you through the Management 3.0 Identity Symbols process: name, symbol, values, and working agreements.

name.title: What is your team's name?
name.subtitle: A good team name is memorable, meaningful, and reflects your shared identity.
name.tip: Tip: avoid generic names like 'Team A'. Choose something that represents your purpose or character.

symbol.title: Choose your team symbol
symbol.subtitle: Pick an animal or archetype that resonates with your team's character, or enter a custom one.
symbol.custom_label: Or use a custom emoji / symbol

values.title: Select your team values
values.subtitle: Pick 3–5 values that matter most to your team. You can add custom ones too.

agreements.title: Working Agreements
agreements.subtitle: Define how you work together. Choose from suggestions or write your own.

learn.title: About Team Identity
learn.why_body: Teams with a shared identity have higher cohesion, clearer decisions, and better onboarding. Management 3.0's Identity Symbols practice helps teams articulate who they are, not just what they do.
learn.expo_body: A Work Expo is a team showcase event where teams present their charter, projects, and working culture to the rest of the organization. It builds cross-team understanding and pride.
